target.name: OpenAI
target.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
OpenAI, a Seed-stage AI & Software company, commands a current post-money valuation of $300B+ following $40B+ in total fundraising. The applicable peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies across AI & Software—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private entities including Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, xAI, and Anthropic, spanning Seed through Series A stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate a defensible range. Stage-Progression analysis applies a typical 2.5x multiplier to the $300B last post-money valuation, implying $750B at the next funding stage. Peer-Median analysis examines five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Thinking Machines Lab at $12.0B, Liquid AI at $2.4B, Field AI at $2.0B, Sakana AI at $1.0B, and Twelve Labs at $250M), yielding a median of $2.0B with a range of $250M to $12.0B. The stage-progression method reflects OpenAI's exceptional scale relative to peers, while the peer-median approach anchors to market transactions, though notably below current valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $1.7B (low) to $862.5B (high), with a median of $376.0B. The material disparity between methods reflects OpenAI's outlier status within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the appropriate discount for Seed stage relative to last financing, the representativeness of the peer sample given OpenAI's disproportionate scale, and whether recent private financing reflects fundamental value or capital availability dynamics specific to large language model infrastructure.
